items.q: What are the four Ps of the marketing mix?
items.correct: Product, Price, Place (distribution), and Promotion — the controllable variables a business uses to satisfy customers and achieve objectives.
items.misconception: The four Ps are Product, Price, Profit, and People.
items.random_wrong: The four Ps are Planning, Producing, Promoting, and Profit.
items.why: Students often substitute 'profit' or 'people' into the four Ps. The classic McCarthy framework is Product, Price, Place and Promotion — extended models add others but those four are foundational.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.correct: An arrangement where a franchisor grants a franchisee the right to use their brand, products and systems in exchange for fees and a share of revenue.
items.misconception: A franchise is when a business owns multiple branches itself.
items.random_wrong: A franchise is when employees buy shares in their company.
items.why: Owned chains are not franchises. A franchise involves separate ownership — McDonald's restaurants are mostly franchised (owned by individual operators), not owned by McDonald's Corporation.

items.q: Which is NOT one of the 4 Ps?
items.why: Correct answer: People. “Product” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Splitting a market by age and income is which type of segmentation?
items.why: Correct answer: Demographic. “Geographic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A firm sells 8,000 units of a £25 product. What is total revenue, in £?
items.why: The real figure is 200000. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
